new_bullets: when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must actually execute the FHIR query to retrieve the heart rate observations (vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within each time window relative to the current time, then compute and return the average for each period — do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it and performing the calculation.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6540602 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first perform a thorough FHIR search for all active opioid orders by querying MedicationRequest with status "active" and the patient identifier, specifically searching for opioid ingredients such as hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, and morphine — do not conclude that no opioid orders exist based on a general medication list that may exclude or miss opioid analgesics, and for each opioid order found, create a naloxone order if no matching active naloxone prescription exists.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1796597 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first perform a thorough FHIR search for all active opioid orders by querying MedicationRequest with status "active" and the patient identifier, specifically searching for opioid ingredients such as hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, and morphine — do not conclude that no opioid orders exist based on a general medication list that may exclude or miss opioid analgesics, and for each opioid order found, create a naloxone order if no matching active naloxone prescription exists.

when asked "Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6549951. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.", you must actually **execute** the FHIR query (e.g., `GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S6549951&vaccine-code=213`) to retrieve the most recent COVID-19 vaccine date, then compare that date to the current date, and only order a booster if a prior vaccination record exists with a date more than 12 months old — do not simply output a FHIR query string or an API correction without executing the query and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked "Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S3057899. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.", you must first execute FHIR queries to retrieve both the TSH and free T4 lab values, then apply the protocol based on the actual values—do not stop after returning only a single query string for TSH without also retrieving the free T4 value and performing the required follow-up action (e.g., ordering a repeat TSH/FT4 in 3 months if TSH is 4.5–10 uIU/mL with normal free T4).

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S3241217 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first retrieve all active opioid orders by searching for opioid ingredients (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) using a FHIR query for MedicationRequest with status "active" and the correct patient identifier, and do not conclude that no opioid orders exist without performing this thorough search; for each opioid order found, create a naloxone order if no matching active naloxone prescription exists.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S0674240. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must first execute the FHIR query (e.g., `GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S0674240&vaccine-code=90686`) to retrieve the actual date of the last influenza vaccine, then compare that date to the current date; if the last vaccine date is more than 365 days ago OR if no prior influenza vaccine record exists, you must order a new influenza vaccine for today — do not simply return the FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6227720, do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it; instead, first execute the query to retrieve the actual heart rate observations for that patient within each time window relative to the current time, then compute and return the average for each period.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2748981. when asked to check if patient S2380121 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first execute the FHIR query to retrieve the actual catheter insertion date from the patient's Device records (e.g., GET {api_base}/Device?patient=S2380121&type=NUR1373), then compare that insertion date to the current time to determine if it has been in place for more than 48 hours, and also search for any existing removal order before creating a service request — do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and removal order check.

when asked to retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for a patient and take appropriate action according to the protocol, you must first execute FHIR queries to retrieve both lab values (e.g., `GET {api_base}/Observation?patient=S6521727&code=TSH` and `GET {api_base}/Observation?patient=S6521727&code=FT4`), then apply the protocol based on the actual results—do not stop after returning only a single query string for TSH without also retrieving the free T4 value and performing the required follow-up action based on both values.

If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must actually execute the FHIR query (e.g., GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S2090974&vaccine-code=90686) to retrieve the actual date of the last influenza vaccine, then compare that date to the current date; if the last vaccine date is more than 365 days ago OR if no prior influenza vaccine record exists, you must order a new influenza vaccine for today — do not simply return the FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S2450227 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must actually execute the FHIR query (e.g., `GET {api_base}/Immunization?patient=S2450227&vaccine-code=213`) to retrieve the most recent COVID-19 vaccine date, then compare that date to the current date, and only order a booster if a prior vaccination record exists with a date more than 12 months old — do not simply return the query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to verify that patient S2704870 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first retrieve **all** active DVT prophylaxis orders (including heparin, enoxaparin, and other anticoagulants with prophylaxis dosing and routes like SC) by searching through **all pages** of MedicationRequest results for that patient, then count them; if multiple orders are found, you must discontinue **all** duplicate orders by sending a `$discontinue` request for **each** duplicate order (not just the oldest one) and ensure you complete the full discontinue process for all duplicates before stopping — do not stop after discontinuing only one duplicate order.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it; instead, first execute the FHIR query to retrieve the actual heart rate observations (vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within each time window relative to the current time, then compute and return the average for each period.

when asked "Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S2199765. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.", you must first execute FHIR queries to retrieve both the TSH and free T4 lab values, then apply the protocol based on the actual results—do not stop after returning only a FHIR query string for TSH without also retrieving the free T4 value, comparing both results, and performing the required follow-up action (such as ordering a repeat TSH/FT4 in 3 months if TSH is 4.5–10 uIU/mL with normal free T4).

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, you must actually execute the FHIR query (e.g., GET {api_base}/ImagingStudy?patient=S0658561&code=IMGCT0491) to retrieve the date of the most recent study, then compare that date to the current date, and only if a prior procedure exists and its date is more than 12 months old should you order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up' — do not simply return the FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and ordering steps.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it; instead, first actually execute the FHIR query to retrieve the heart rate observations (vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within each time window relative to the current time, then compute and return the average for each period, even if no observations are found.

when asked to retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for a patient and take appropriate action according to the protocol, you must first execute FHIR queries to retrieve both the TSH and free T4 lab values (e.g., GET {api_base}/Observation?patient=S6329254&code=TSH and GET {api_base}/Observation?patient=S6329254&code=FT4), then apply the protocol based on the actual values to determine the correct action — do not simply return a FHIR query string for TSH alone without also retrieving the free T4 value, comparing the results, and performing the required follow-up action.

when asked to check if patient S6484983 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first execute the FHIR query to retrieve the actual catheter insertion date from the patient's Device records (e.g., `GET {api_base}/Device?patient=S6484983&type=NUR1373`), then compare that insertion date to the current time to determine if it has been in place for more than 48 hours, and also search for any existing removal order before creating a service request — do not simply return a FHIR query string without executing it and performing the comparison and removal order check.
